varieties.name: Yellow Sweet Cassava
varieties.origin: Heirloom variety, cultivated in Australia for decades
varieties.traits: Perennial shrub, 1-3m tall, drought-tolerant once established
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, sweet variety with low cyanide content
varieties.growing_notes: ONLY sweet cassava permitted for food in Australia per FSANZ regulations. Excellent subtropical adaptation. Thrives in Brisbane's warm humid climate. Perfect potato substitute for chips, fries, flour. Tolerates full sun to partial shade. Requires well-drained soil.
varieties.availability: Food Forest Seeds Australia (QLD), Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Heaven On Earth Fruit Trees, various Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: White Sweet Cassava
varieties.origin: Traditional variety, long cultivation history in Queensland
varieties.traits: Woody shrub, 2-3m height, drought-resistant
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, bland satisfying taste when cooked, sweet variety
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, tubers best dug when small

varieties.name: Pink Flesh Cassava
varieties.origin: Australian collection, grown by Queensland gardeners
varieties.traits: Moderate growth, distinctive coloring
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink-tinged flesh, unique among Australian varieties

varieties.name: Manihot esculenta 'Variegata'
varieties.origin: Ornamental selection, grown in Brisbane gardens
varieties.traits: Variegated foliage, decorative and edible, 1.5-2.5m
varieties.flesh_fruit: Edible roots, leaves also consumed after cooking
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, primarily grown for ornamental value
varieties.growing_notes: Listed in Brisbane Edible Gardening subtropical plant list. Both roots and leaves edible when properly prepared. Attractive garden specimen. Tolerates Brisbane's humidity. Suitable for edible landscaping.
varieties.availability: Brisbane area specialty nurseries, permaculture suppliers

varieties.name: MAus Collection Varieties
varieties.origin: University of Queensland Australian Cassava Collection, collected from North Queensland 1972-1990s, unknown international origin
varieties.traits: Various growth habits, adapted to Queensland conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: White to cream flesh, variable root characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Variable, collected from private gardens and farms

varieties.name: CUQ6 (Fiji)
varieties.origin: Collected at Hambeldon Sugar Mill, Cairns 1972, believed from Fiji
varieties.traits: High-yielding vegetative growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality storage roots
varieties.yield_notes: High root production across range of Australian environments
varieties.growing_notes: Widely distributed in Australia, sent to Africa. Excellent performance in Queensland conditions. Maintained in Australian Cassava Collection at UQ Redland Bay near Brisbane. Proven subtropical adaptation. Now growing in Queensland and at research facilities.
varieties.availability: Research collections, AgSight, limited commercial availability

varieties.name: CIAT Introduction Varieties
varieties.origin: International Center for Tropical Agriculture (CIAT), Colombia, introduced to Australia ~1980
varieties.traits: Varying leaf cyanide content (low to high), diverse characteristics
varieties.flesh_fruit: Variable root quality and characteristics
varieties.growing_notes: Maintained in Australian Cassava collection at UQ Redland Bay farm near Brisbane. Stem cuttings sent to ANU Canberra ~1990, later to AgSight 2016. Established in field nursery at Kununurra WA, also growing in Queensland and Monash University Melbourne. Provide genetic diversity for Brisbane-area cassava cultivation.

varieties.name: Puerto Rico Collection (via Hawaii)
varieties.origin: Introduced from Puerto Rico via Hawaii to UQ 1972
varieties.traits: Retained original cultivar names in Australian collection
varieties.flesh_fruit: Variable characteristics from Caribbean germplasm
varieties.growing_notes: First international introduction to Australian Cassava Collection. Brought subtropical-adapted Caribbean germplasm to Queensland. Historic importance for Australian cassava development. Suitable for Brisbane climate zone with adequate moisture.

varieties.name: Nagasakiwase
varieties.origin: Asian variety grown in Australian subtropics
varieties.traits: Unique 2 crops per year in subtropics capability
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, improved with summer cincturing
varieties.yield_notes: First crop April/May, second in August. Much larger August crop with better fruit size using cincturing technique

varieties.name: ACP444
varieties.origin: Australian Cassava Products (Fielders/CSR) selection 1978-1984, Bundaberg region
varieties.traits: Selected from F1 seedlings of various crosses
varieties.flesh_fruit: High-quality roots
varieties.yield_notes: Superior yield documented in research references
varieties.growing_notes: Historic Queensland breeding program product. Developed in Bundaberg (near Brisbane) for Australian conditions. Superior performance noted in 1978-84 trials. Current status unclear but represents important Queensland breeding history. Adapted to subtropical climate.

varieties.name: M4 (Malayan-4) 🏆
varieties.origin: Introduced from Malaysia 1950s, naturalized in Kerala

varieties.name: Sree Prakash 🏆
varieties.origin: CTCRI Trivandrum, indigenous clonal selection, released 1980s-90s
varieties.traits: Short, non-branching, early maturing 7-8 months, high leaf retention, highly tolerant to Cercospora leaf spot
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good cooking quality, popular in low-lying areas

varieties.name: Sree Jaya 🏆
varieties.origin: CTCRI, indigenous germplasm clonal selection
varieties.traits: Medium height, short-duration 6 months, susceptible to CMD
varieties.flesh_fruit: Brown skin, purple rind, white flesh, good cooking quality, conical roots

varieties.name: Sree Vijaya 🏆
varieties.origin: CTCRI, indigenous germplasm clonal selection
varieties.traits: Short-duration 6-7 months, susceptible to mite and scale insect
varieties.flesh_fruit: Cream-colored rind, light yellow flesh due to high carotene content, conical roots

varieties.name: H-97
varieties.origin: CTCRI 1971, hybrid between Manjavella Local (Kerala) and Brazilian selection
varieties.traits: Erect branching habit, light sepia colored emerging leaves, CMD tolerant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Brown tubers, good quality
varieties.growing_notes: One of three historic 1971 CTCRI releases. Disease tolerance important for Kerala's humid monsoon climate. Combines Kerala local adaptation with Brazilian vigor. Suitable for both Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Recommended for CMD-prone areas including Kerala lowlands.
varieties.availability: CTCRI, Kerala and Tamil Nadu agricultural universities

varieties.name: H-226
varieties.origin: CTCRI 1971, hybrid between local Kerala cultivar and Malayan M4
varieties.traits: Semi-branching, characteristic green leaves, susceptible to CMD
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light purple rind, cream skin with purple patches

varieties.name: Sree Sahya (H-2304)
varieties.origin: CTCRI, multiple hybrid involving five parents (two exotic, three indigenous Kerala)
varieties.traits: Non-branching, dark brown petiole, spiny prominent stipular mark
varieties.flesh_fruit: Long-necked roots, cream-colored rind, broad thick dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: 35-40 t/ha in 10-11 months, very compact root system

varieties.name: Sree Harsha
varieties.origin: CTCRI, first triploid hybrid (2n=54), crossing diploid with induced tetraploid clone of Sree Sahya
varieties.traits: Stout, erect, non-branching, shade-tolerant, palatable, drought-tolerant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good cooking quality tubers, white flesh
varieties.yield_notes: 35-60 t/ha in 7-8 months, exceptionally high starch 38-41%

varieties.name: CO-1
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University Coimbatore, clonal selection
varieties.traits: Clonal selection with stable characteristics
varieties.flesh_fruit: Whitish brown skin, creamy rind, 35% starch content

varieties.name: CO-2
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University
varieties.traits: Branching variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Brown skin, creamy white rind, 34.6% starch

varieties.name: CO-3
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, early maturing clone
varieties.traits: Branching variety, popular in various Tamil Nadu districts
varieties.flesh_fruit: Brown skin, 35.6% starch content
varieties.yield_notes: 32-42.6 t/ha in 7.5-8 months, very high productivity

varieties.name: CO (Tp) 4
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, selection from Me501
varieties.traits: Field tolerant to red spider mite and scales, excellent growth vigor
varieties.flesh_fruit: High quality, 40% starch content - highest among CO varieties

varieties.name: CO (Tp) 5
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, seedling selection released 2007
varieties.traits: Resistant to CMD, suitable for irrigated conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: 26% starch content
varieties.growing_notes: Disease resistance important for Kerala's humid climate. Recent release with good adaptation. Suitable for irrigated cultivation in Kerala. CMD resistance valuable for monsoon-prone areas. Can be cultivated with supplemental irrigation during dry spells.
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, Kerala agricultural departments

varieties.name: MVD-1 (Mulluvadi) 🏆
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu release 1993, traditional variety naturalized in region
varieties.traits: Excellent nutritional profile in leaves and roots
varieties.flesh_fruit: High quality, highest protein, carbohydrate, NPK, minerals. Lowest tannins and HCN among tested varieties

varieties.name: Nidhi 🌱
varieties.origin: Kerala Agricultural University, clonal selection for central Kerala
varieties.traits: Grayish white stem, short duration 5-6 months, mosaic tolerant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Suited to sandy loam soils of central Kerala
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically developed for central Kerala conditions including Thiruvalla region. Very short duration allows quick turnover. Mosaic tolerance important for disease-prone areas. Adapted to specific soil types of central Kerala. Excellent for local farming systems.
varieties.availability: Kerala Agricultural University, central Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: Vellayani Hraswa 🌱
varieties.origin: Kerala Agricultural University Vellayani, recent selection
varieties.traits: Short duration 5-6 months, excellent cooking quality
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink tuber rind, superior palatability
varieties.yield_notes: 44.01 t/ha - exceptionally high for short duration
varieties.growing_notes: Outstanding combination of short duration and high yield. Developed specifically for Kerala conditions. Excellent cooking quality makes it ideal for food use. Perfect for paddy fallow in Kerala lowlands. Very suitable for Thiruvalla and southern Kerala areas.
varieties.availability: Kerala Agricultural University Vellayani campus, Kerala state nurseries

varieties.name: Sree Rekha
varieties.origin: CTCRI, superior top-cross hybrid from inbreds
varieties.traits: Heterotic variety, excellent vigor
varieties.flesh_fruit: High yield with good culinary quality

varieties.name: Sree Prabha
varieties.origin: CTCRI, superior top-cross hybrid from inbreds
varieties.traits: Heterotic variety with hybrid vigor
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good culinary quality combined with high yield
varieties.growing_notes: Recently developed, ready for formal release. Top-cross hybrid breeding provides excellent characteristics. Suitable for Kerala monsoon conditions. Good for both food and potential industrial use. Expanding availability in Kerala and Tamil Nadu.

varieties.name: Sree Athulya
varieties.origin: CTCRI 2013, triploid hybrid
varieties.traits: Triploid vigor, excellent growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: High extractable starch content >30%
varieties.growing_notes: Second triploid release after Sree Harsha. High starch makes valuable for industry. Triploid breeding provides enhanced characteristics. Suitable for Kerala's diverse farming systems. Recent release with good adaptation potential.
varieties.availability: CTCRI, expanding in Kerala and Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: Sree Apoorva
varieties.origin: CTCRI 2013, triploid hybrid
varieties.traits: Triploid variety with enhanced vigor
varieties.flesh_fruit: High extractable starch content >30%

varieties.name: Sree Raksha (CR24-4)
varieties.origin: CIAT introduction, recently released by CTCRI for Kerala
varieties.traits: Higher CMD resistance, post-harvest deterioration tolerance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality with extended shelf life

varieties.name: Sree Pavithra
varieties.origin: CTCRI recent release, nutrient-efficient variety
varieties.traits: Nutrient-efficient, suitable for lower fertility soils
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality with efficient nutrient use
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields even with reduced inputs
varieties.growing_notes: Important advancement for sustainable cultivation. Nutrient efficiency reduces input costs for farmers. Suitable for Kerala's marginally fertile soils. Recent release with modern breeding advantages. Valuable for small-holder farmers in Kerala.

varieties.name: Yethapur 1 (YTP 1)
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University Tapioca and Castor Research Station, Yethapur
varieties.traits: Developed at Salem district research station
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality for local conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Adapted to Tamil Nadu/Kerala border region

varieties.name: CI-649, CI-731, CI-732 🏆
varieties.origin: CTCRI indigenous clones, short-duration selections
varieties.traits: Early maturing, good cooking quality
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality characteristics for table use
varieties.yield_notes: Short duration varieties allowing multiple crops

varieties.name: H-119
varieties.origin: CTCRI Trivandrum, early maturing clone
varieties.traits: Short duration 7.5-8 months, early maturing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, ~30% starch content

varieties.name: ME-46 and ME-10
varieties.origin: TNAU selections for coastal South Arcot district
varieties.traits: Coastal area adaptation
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality for coastal conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Outstanding performance in coastal areas near Kerala border. Under further evaluation for wider release. High yields make them promising. Suitable for coastal Kerala areas with similar conditions. Important for expanding cassava to coastal zones.

varieties.name: All varieties are Non-GMO | Heirloom varieties highlighted with
